If the snoring becomes very loud, the bear raises its hand to touch the sleeper's face, changing their position without waking them. The passage also mentions that this is the first anti-snoring machine and that it tracks blood oxygen levels using a monitor that the user has to insert their finger into. So now, let's look at each option. Option A: It's comfortable to sleep on. The passage doesn't mention anything about the comfort of the pillow. The passage says that the bear touches the face to change the sleeper's position without waking them. So B is incorrect because the purpose is to stop snoring without waking them up. Option C: It's the first machine made to deal with snoring. The passage says, "This is definitely the world's first anti-snoring machine..." So this seems correct. The passage directly states that it's the first of its kind. Option D: It uses a monitor to detect the loudness of the snoring.
